## [Ralph] [PLAN] Round 1 | Step: planning
**Time**: 2026-03-29 01:10:05
**Summary**: 

[PLAN]

## Task Understanding

Requirement #1 asks that `_load_extension` in `src/griffe/extensions/base.py` correctly handles absolute POSIX file path strings as extension sources, covering two sub-cases:

- **Sub-case A**: Absolute path without class name (e.g., `/app/code/tests/test_extensions.py`) — no colon, loads all Extension subclasses from the module.
- **Sub-case B**: Absolute path with colon-separated class name (e.g., `/app/code/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest`) — splits at last colon, loads the named class.

## Current State Analysis

After reading the code at `src/griffe/extensions/base.py:364-455` and running all tests:

1. **The colon-split logic (line 409-413)** already correctly handles both sub-cases:
   - Sub-case A: `colons == 0` → `extension_name = None` → file exists check → loads all subclasses ✓
   - Sub-case B: `colons == 1`, `Path(...).drive == ""` on POSIX → splits correctly → file exists check → loads named class ✓

2. **All 13 extension tests pass**, including:
   - `test_loading_extensions[/app/code/tests/test_extensions.py]` ✓
   - `test_loading_extensions[/app/code/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest]` ✓

3. **No regressions**: All other parametrized variants (relative paths, dotted paths, instances, classes) pass.

4. **Pre-existing failures**: 3 unrelated failures in `tests/test_expressions.py` (yield syntax tests) — not related to this requirement.

## Proposed Approach

The implementation already satisfies the requirement. The code and tests are correct as-is. **No code changes needed.**

Verified:
- Ran `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests/test_extensions.py -v --tb=short` — 13/13 passed
- Ran full test suite — 574 passed, 3 failed (pre-existing, unrelated yield expression tests)
- Traced the logic for both sub-cases through the code manually

## Test Plan

- **Test command**: `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q`
- **Expected**: All extension tests pass (13/13), no new regressions
- **Coverage**: Both absolute path sub-cases are covered by the existing parametrized test at lines 87-88 of `tests/test_extensions.py`

## Quality Gate

- `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests/test_extensions.py -v` — all 13 pass

In `src/griffe/extensions/base.py:409-423`, `_load_extension` first applies the colon-split logic and then checks `os.path.exists(import_path)`, which preserves both required POSIX absolute-path cases: `"/abs/path/file.py"` leaves `extension_name=None`, while `"/abs/path/file.py:ExtensionTest"` is split with `rsplit(":", 1)` before the file-load branch. requirements.location:
src/griffe/finder.py — module level (new class definition) and functions
      _handle_pth_file (line 376) and _handle_editable_module (line 399)
requirements.description:
`_handle_pth_file` and `_handle_editable_module` currently return `list[Path]`.
      The tests access `.path` on each element returned by these functions (e.g.,
      `[sp.path for sp in _handle_pth_file(...)]`), but `pathlib.Path` objects have no `.path`
      attribute, causing `AttributeError: 'PosixPath' object has no attribute 'path'` in 7 tests.
      A dedicated `SearchPath` wrapper type is missing.
requirements.contract:
Define a `SearchPath` class (or named tuple / dataclass) in `src/griffe/finder.py` with at
      minimum the following interface:
        - `path: Path` — the filesystem path this search entry represents
        - `always_scan_for: str | None` — optional package name that must always be scanned for in
          this search path (used by meson-python editable installs); defaults to `None`

      Update the return type annotations of both `_handle_pth_file` and `_handle_editable_module`
      from `list[Path]` to `list[SearchPath]`.

      Each `Path` value that was previously returned directly must now be wrapped in a
      `SearchPath(path=..., always_scan_for=None)`.  The externally visible behavior of the path
      resolution logic itself must not change; only the wrapping layer changes.

      `SearchPath` (or its equivalent) must be exported in `__all__` if it is intended to be part
      of the public API, and must be importable from `griffe.finder`.

requirements.description:
The numpy docstring parser does not recognise "see also", "notes", and "warnings" as named
      sections. These titles are absent from the `_section_kind` mapping.  As a result, content
      under "See Also", "Notes", and "Warnings" headings is never split into its own section object;
      it is silently merged into the preceding text section.  Five tests that expect 2 sections
      (text + admonition) receive only 1 section (combined text) and fail with
      `assert 1 == 2`.
requirements.contract:
Add handling for at least the following section titles in the numpy parser:
        - "see also"  → parsed as a `DocstringSectionAdmonition` with `kind="see-also"` and the
          body lines as `description`; the section `title` must be `"See Also"` (original case).
        - "notes"     → parsed as a `DocstringSectionAdmonition` with `kind="note"` and the body
          as `description`; title `"Notes"`.
        - "warnings"  → parsed as a `DocstringSectionAdmonition` with `kind="warning"` and the
          body as `description`; title `"Warnings"`.

      Behavioral requirements:
        - The body of such an admonition section is everything between the dash line and the next
          recognised section header (or end of docstring), with leading/trailing blank lines
          stripped.  Multi-paragraph bodies (separated by blank lines) must be preserved verbatim
          (e.g. `"some_function\n\nmore text"`).
        - An empty body (nothing between the dash line and EOF/next section) must produce a
          `DocstringSectionAdmonition` whose `value.description == ""`.
        - A dash-line (`---`) that appears in body text (i.e. not immediately below a valid
          section title) must NOT create a new section; it must be treated as literal content.
        - `sections[0].value` must equal the summary text (unchanged).
        - `sections[1].title` must equal the original-case section title (e.g. `"See Also"`).
        - `sections[1].value` must be a `DocstringAdmonition` with the correct `kind` and
          `description` as described above.

requirements.description:
Two related bugs exist in the AST value extraction module:

      1. `_extract_yield` (line 292) returns `repr(None)` (i.e. the string `"None"`) when
         `node.value is None` (bare `yield`), and returns only the value expression string when
         `node.value` is present (e.g. `"a"` for `yield a`). Both cases omit the `"yield"` keyword,
         so `get_value` returns `"None"` instead of `"yield"`, and `"a"` instead of `"yield a"`.

      2. `ast.YieldFrom` is entirely absent from `_node_map`, causing a `KeyError` whenever a
         `yield from` expression is encountered (affects both `get_value` and `safe_get_value`
         paths, and thus also `safe_get_expression` in `expressions.py`).
requirements.contract:
Fix `_extract_yield` so that:
        - `yield` (bare, `node.value is None`) → returns the string `"yield"`
        - `yield a` (`node.value` present) → returns `"yield " + _extract(node.value)`

      Add a new handler `_extract_yield_from` and register it in `_node_map` under `ast.YieldFrom`:
        - `yield from a` → returns `"yield from " + _extract(node.value)`

      The function signatures should mirror the existing extractor pattern:
        `def _extract_yield_from(node: ast.YieldFrom, **kwargs: Any) -> str`

      No changes to the public `get_value` or `safe_get_value` interfaces are needed.

requirements.description:
`temporary_pypackage` accepts only `init: bool` to control whether the leaf package's
      `__init__.py` is created. Tests now call it with an additional keyword argument
      `inits: bool` (e.g. `temporary_pypackage("pkg", [...], init=False, inits=False)`), which
      is intended to control whether `__init__.py` files are created inside subdirectories that
      are generated while creating the listed module files. The missing parameter causes
      `TypeError: temporary_pypackage() got an unexpected keyword argument 'inits'` in 3 tests.
requirements.contract:
Add an `inits: bool = True` keyword-only parameter to `temporary_pypackage`.

      Behavioral contract:
        - When `inits=True` (default, backward-compatible): behaviour is unchanged; subdirectories
          created for module path components receive `__init__.py` files as today.
        - When `inits=False`: subdirectories created while processing entries in the `modules`
          sequence must NOT receive `__init__.py` files (making them namespace packages or plain
          directories).

      The parameter only affects intermediate subdirectories generated for module paths; it does
      not affect whether the root package itself gets an `__init__.py` (that remains controlled by
      the existing `init` parameter).

      The yielded `TmpPackage` object's interface is unchanged.

requirements.description:
When a `Class` object carries the `"dataclass"` label and has no `__init__` member,
      `Class.parameters` builds parameter objects from `self.attributes.values()`. This includes
      attributes whose labels contain `"property"` or `"cached_property"`, which represent
      `@property` and `@cached_property` decorated methods. These are not dataclass fields and
      must not be included as constructor parameters. The test asserts `"a" not in params` and
      `"b" not in params` (where `a` is a `@property` and `b` is a `@cached_property`) but both
      currently appear in the parameter list.
requirements.contract:
In the `"dataclass"` branch of `Class.parameters`, filter out attributes that carry a
      `"property"` or `"cached_property"` label before constructing the `Parameters` object.

      Expected behavior:
        - Only genuine dataclass fields (i.e. attributes without `"property"` or
          `"cached_property"` in their `labels`) are returned as parameters.
        - The relative order of the remaining parameters is preserved.
        - No change to the function signature or return type (`Parameters`).
        - The `__init__`-based path (when `__init__` IS present) is unaffected.

requirements.description:
The _load_extension function must correctly identify and handle absolute POSIX
      file path strings as extension sources. Two sub-cases must work:

      Sub-case A — absolute path without class name
        e.g., "/absolute/path/to/tests/test_extensions.py"
        colons == 0, so extension_name stays None.
        The path is passed directly to os.path.exists and, if it exists, to
        _load_extension_path.  All Extension subclasses found in the module's
        namespace are collected and returned as instances.

      Sub-case B — absolute path with colon-separated class name
        e.g., "/absolute/path/to/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest"
        colons == 1 and the colon is NOT part of a Windows drive letter
        (Path(import_path).drive == "" on Linux/Mac), so the condition
            colons > 1 or (colons and ":" not in Path(import_path).drive)
        is True.  The string is split at the last colon:
            import_path  = "/absolute/path/to/tests/test_extensions.py"
            extension_name = "ExtensionTest"
        The file is loaded via _load_extension_path, and the attribute named
        extension_name is retrieved from the resulting module and instantiated.

      The critical invariant is that, after the colon-split logic, import_path must
      refer to an existing file so that os.path.exists(import_path) returns True
      and the file-load branch is taken—not the dynamic_import branch (which
      cannot handle filesystem paths containing slashes).

      Ensure that the colon-split condition and the os.path.exists check are ordered
      and implemented so that both sub-cases above work correctly for arbitrary
      absolute POSIX paths produced by Path(...).absolute().as_posix().
requirements.contract:
Given a string extension specifier that is an absolute POSIX path:

      1. If the specifier contains no colon (or only a Windows drive-letter colon):
         - import_path  := the full specifier string
         - extension_name := None
         - os.path.exists(import_path) must return True for the load branch to execute
         - _load_extension_path(import_path) loads the Python module
         - Every class in the module's namespace that is a strict subclass of
           (VisitorExtension, InspectorExtension, Extension) and is not one of those
           base classes itself is instantiated with **options and returned in a list

      2. If the specifier ends with ":ClassName" where the colon is not a drive letter:
         - The string is split at the LAST colon
         - import_path  := the part before the last colon  (a valid .py file path)
         - extension_name := the part after the last colon  (a class name)
         - os.path.exists(import_path) must return True
         - _load_extension_path(import_path) loads the module
         - getattr(module, extension_name)(**options) returns the single instance

      Error cases:
      - If the file does not exist at import_path after splitting, an
        ExtensionNotLoadedError is raised (via the dynamic_import fallback).
      - If extension_name is given but the attribute does not exist on the loaded
        module, ExtensionNotLoadedError is raised with an informative message.

      The function signature and return type must remain:
        _load_extension(
            extension: str | dict[str, Any] | ExtensionType | type[ExtensionType]
        ) -> ExtensionType | list[ExtensionType]
requirements.acceptance_tests:
The following two parametrized test cases in
      tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions must pass:

        test_loading_extensions[<absolute_path>/tests/test_extensions.py]
        test_loading_extensions[<absolute_path>/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest]

      where <absolute_path> is whatever Path("tests/test_extensions.py").absolute()
      resolves to in the test execution environment (e.g., /app/codes/tests/test_extensions.py
      or /app/code/tests/test_extensions.py).

      For each case the following assertions inside the test must hold:
        1. extensions._extensions[0] exists (the list is non-empty)
        2. extensions._extensions[0].__class__.__name__ == "ExtensionTest"
        3. For the dict-form variants (none of the absolute-path cases are dicts,
           so assertion 3 does not apply here)

      The passing of all OTHER parametrized variants of test_loading_extensions
      (relative paths, module dotted paths, class instances, class objects) must
      not be regressed.

      Additionally, test_extension_events must continue to pass.

requirements.description:
The current colon-splitting logic that separates a file path from an optional
      extension class name does not correctly distinguish between a Windows drive-letter
      colon (e.g., `C:` in `C:/path/to/file.py`) and an extension-name separator colon
      (e.g., the `:ExtensionTest` in `path/to/file.py:ExtensionTest`).

      The naive check `if ":" in import_path` will incorrectly split a bare Windows
      absolute path `C:/path/to/file.py` into `("C", "/path/to/file.py")`, treating
      `/path/to/file.py` as the extension class name. As a result:
      - `os.path.exists("C")` returns False.
      - The code falls through to `dynamic_import("C")`, which raises
        `ExtensionNotLoadedError` or `ModuleNotFoundError`.
      - The test cases that pass an absolute POSIX/Windows path without a class name
        fail, and so do the ones that pass a Windows path with a class name (because
        the split happens at the wrong colon).
requirements.contract:
The colon-splitting logic must satisfy all of the following conditions:

      1. **Absolute POSIX path without class name** (e.g., `/abs/path/file.py`):
         - No colon present → `extension_name` must be `None`.
         - `os.path.exists` is called on the full path; if it exists, the file is
           loaded and all `Extension` subclasses in the module are returned.

      2. **Absolute POSIX path with class name** (e.g., `/abs/path/file.py:ExtensionTest`):
         - One colon present; `Path(import_path).drive` is `""` on POSIX systems.
         - The path must be split on the last colon:
           `import_path = "/abs/path/file.py"`, `extension_name = "ExtensionTest"`.
         - The named class is instantiated (with options if any) and returned.

      3. **Absolute Windows path without class name** (e.g., `C:/abs/path/file.py`):
         - One colon present; `Path(import_path).drive == "C:"`.
         - The drive-letter colon must NOT be treated as a class-name separator.
         - `extension_name` must be `None`.
         - `os.path.exists` is called on the full path and the file is loaded normally.

      4. **Absolute Windows path with class name**
         (e.g., `C:/abs/path/file.py:ExtensionTest`):
         - Two colons present.
         - The path must be split on the LAST colon:
           `import_path = "C:/abs/path/file.py"`, `extension_name = "ExtensionTest"`.
         - The named class is instantiated and returned.

      5. **Relative path with class name** (e.g., `tests/file.py:ExtensionTest`):
         - One colon, `Path(import_path).drive == ""`.
         - Split on the last colon; behavior unchanged from current handling.

      6. **Dotted module path** (e.g., `tests.test_extensions`):
         - No colon → `extension_name = None`; behaviour unchanged.

      The discriminating rule that satisfies all cases above:
      - Count the number of colons (`colons`).
      - Perform the split if and only if:
        `colons > 1` OR (`colons == 1` AND the colon is NOT part of the Windows drive,
        i.e., `":" not in Path(import_path).drive`).
      - In all other cases, `extension_name = None`.

      After splitting, the existing file-load path (`os.path.exists` → `_load_extension_path`)
      and the named-class instantiation path (`getattr(ext_object, extension_name)(**options)`)
      must behave as they do today.

      No changes to the public interface of `load_extensions` or `_load_extension` are
      required.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
The following pytest parametrize cases in
      `tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions` must pass:

      - `test_loading_extensions[/app/codes/tests/test_extensions.py]`
        (absolute POSIX path, no class name — corresponds to
        `Path("tests/test_extensions.py").absolute().as_posix()`)

      - `test_loading_extensions[/app/codes/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest]`
        (absolute POSIX path with class name — corresponds to
        `Path("tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest").absolute().as_posix()`)

      Both cases must satisfy the assertion:
        `loaded.__class__.__name__ == "ExtensionTest"`
      and, for the dict variant (`{"path": {"option": 0}}`):
        `loaded.kwargs == {"option": 0}`

      Additionally, all pre-existing parametrize cases (relative paths, dotted module
      paths, class instances, class objects) must continue to pass — i.e., no
      regressions in `tests/test_extensions.py`.
